experiment: F-CAT1 FMEA refresh — S403 (22 sessions post-S381)
expect: >=1 new FM since S381 (NAT ~S427). >=2 MINIMAL->ADEQUATE upgrades. FMEA artifact updated.
actual: 3 new FMs identified (FM-15/FM-16/FM-17). 2 MINIMAL->ADEQUATE upgrades (FM-14 via check.sh git fsck, FM-09 reclassification). All 14 prior defense layers INTACT. No catastrophic incidents S381-S403.
diff: Expected >=1 new FM by ~S427 — got 3 new FMs at S403 (24 sessions EARLIER than NAT prediction). Expected >=2 upgrades — got exactly 2 (CONFIRMED). Did NOT predict the new FMs would be system-level (signals, dispatch, metadata) rather than infrastructure (git, WSL). NAT cycle pattern shifts from infrastructure to system-design layer.

severity_definitions.1: Catastrophic — permanent data loss or swarm-breaking corruption
severity_definitions.2: Serious — data corruption or major state inconsistency (recoverable)
severity_definitions.3: Moderate — work duplication, minor corruption, auto-recoverable

changes_since_s381: FM-09: MINIMAL->ADEQUATE (reclassification — 3 layers, 2 automated already existed at S381)
FM-14: MINIMAL->ADEQUATE (check.sh git fsck guard added S403)
FM-15: NEW (zero-entropy field masking, severity 3, MINIMAL)
FM-16: NEW (silent proxy-K threshold, severity 2, MINIMAL)
FM-17: NEW (dispatch-frontier precision gap, severity 3, MINIMAL)
registry_summary.defense_layer_audit: All 14 S381 defense layers INTACT. 0 degraded by S402 tool consolidation (14 archived tools had 0 FMEA layer references). f_con3_constitution_monitor.py deleted S359 — partial gap (CLAUDE.md/PHILOSOPHY.md not hash-protected, only CORE.md covered by genesis hash).
registry_summary.nat_prediction_status: NAT S381 predicted next FM by ~S430. Actual: 3 new FMs at S403 (27 sessions early). Pattern shift: FMs moved from infrastructure (git/WSL/filesystem) to system-design (signals/dispatch/metadata). Next NAT: ~S453 (50 sessions from S403).

failure_modes.name: Concurrent deletion storm
failure_modes.note: S381 rated MINIMAL despite meeting ADEQUATE threshold (3 layers, 2 automated: check.sh FM-09 NOTICE at threshold=5, orient.py check_foreign_staged_deletions(), rule L-346). Corrected.

failure_modes.name: WSL loose object corruption
failure_modes.gap: Auto-detection only; no auto-repair. Recovery still manual.

failure_modes.name: Zero-entropy field masking
failure_modes.mechanism: Cargo-cult fields (available=yes, blocked=none, human_open_item=none) carry 100% default values. Zero information entropy. Creates false safety signal (blocking never reported) and generates false DUE cascades when enforcement checks require the fields. 3 commits blocked in S400-S401.

layers.layer: L-858 identification + L-703 field removal from open_lane.py
maintenance.py REQUIRED field enforcement (catches missing fields)

failure_modes.gap: Fields still present in existing lanes. maintenance.py enforces presence not value. No entropy check.

failure_modes.name: Silent threshold (concurrent-state gating)
failure_modes.mechanism: proxy-K 10% URGENT threshold fires 0 times across 30 sessions. Gated behind clean-tree condition (maintenance.py:1625) that is never true during concurrent operation (56/58 sessions dirty). Actual compaction at 60-70% drift (7x higher than policy). 257-session gap between threshold crossing and first compaction.
failure_modes.gap: Alert delivery mechanism silenced by structural precondition. Threshold exists but never fires. Design-time assumption (clean trees) violated by production regime (always concurrent).

failure_modes.name: Dispatch-frontier precision gap (novelty bias)
failure_modes.mechanism: Domain-level dispatch 80% accurate but frontier-level precision was 0% (S396-S400). Nodes enter COMMIT domains correctly but choose first-wave explorations over stuck campaigns. 5th escalation (orient.py stall detection, S401) improved to 27.8% (L-866). Serial dependency chains create infinite-hardening loops (social-media: 5 lanes invested, 0 executed due to SIG-38 blocking).
failure_modes.gap: 27.8% precision up from 0% but still 72% miss rate. No execution_blocked detection for serial dependency chains.

nat_analysis.s381_prediction: Next FM by ~S430 from infrastructure layer
nat_analysis.s403_result: 3 new FMs at S403 — 27 sessions early
nat_analysis.pattern_shift: S302-S381 FMs were infrastructure (git, WSL, filesystem). S403 FMs are system-design (signals, dispatch, metadata). As infrastructure hardens, failure modes migrate up the abstraction stack.
nat_analysis.next_prediction: Next FM by ~S453. Most probable vectors: (1) measurement instrument decay (L-820 stale observers), (2) protocol format evolution (delimiter/regex mismatch as system grows), (3) knowledge structure corruption (citation graph integrity at 800+ lessons).
nat_analysis.nat_accuracy: 3/3 NAT cycles predicted FM emergence correctly. Timing predictions: S351 (predicted S401, actual S377, 24s early), S377 (predicted S427, actual S403, 24s early). NAT overestimates inter-FM intervals by ~24 sessions. Adjusted prediction: ~S430 (not S453).

hardening_next.action: Fix maintenance.py: remove clean-tree gating on proxy-K URGENT or switch to HEAD-based measurement (L-849 prescription)
Add automated git index health check (both layers currently rule-only; easiest remaining sev-2 upgrade)
Add execution_blocked detection: if frontier has SIG-XX dependency, skip dispatch to that frontier and route to the dependency instead
Remove cargo-cult fields from open_lane.py and maintenance.py REQUIRED list (L-858 prescription)
